title: The Old Keg Of Rum
content:
آ آ آ آ My name is old Jack Palmer,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m a man of olden days,
آ آ آ آ And so I wish to sing a song
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To you of olden praise.
آ آ آ آ To tell of merry friends of old
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ When we were gay and young;
آ آ آ آ How we sat and sang together
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Round the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  How we sat and sang together
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Round the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ There was I and Jack the plough-boy,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Jem Moore and old Tom Hines,
آ آ آ آ And poor old Tom the fiddler,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Who now in glory shines;

آ آ آ آ And several more of our old chums,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Who shine in Kingdom Come,
آ آ آ آ We all associated round the
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  We all associated round the
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ And when harvest time was over,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And weï؟½d get our harvest fee,
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½d meet, and quickly rise the keg,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And then weï؟½d have a spree.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½d sit and sing together
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Till we got that blind and dumb
آ آ آ آ That we couldnï؟½t find the bunghole
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Of the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  That we couldnï؟½t find the bunghole
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Of the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ Its jovially together, boysï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Weï؟½d laugh, weï؟½d chat, weï؟½d sing;
آ آ آ آ Sometimes weï؟½d have a little row
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Some argument would bring.

آ آ آ آ And oftimes in a scrimmage, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve corked it with my thumb,
آ آ آ آ To keep the life from leaking
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ From the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  To keep the life from leaking
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  From the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ But when our spree was ended, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And waking from a snooze,
آ آ آ آ For to give another drain
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The old keg would refuse.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½d rap it with our knucklesï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ If it sounded like a drum,
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½d know the life and spirit
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Had left the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Weï؟½d know the life and spirit
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Had left the Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ Those happy days have passed away,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve seen their pleasures fade;
آ آ آ آ And many of our good old friends
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Have with old times decayed.

آ آ آ آ But still, when on my travels, boys,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ If I meet with an old chum,
آ آ آ آ We will sigh, in conversation,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Of the Grand Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  We will sigh, in conversation,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Of the Grand Old Keg of Rum.

آ آ آ آ So now, kind friends, I end my song,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I hope weï؟½ll meet again,
آ آ آ آ And, as Iï؟½ve tried to please you all,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I hope you wonï؟½t complain.
آ آ آ آ You younger folks who learn my song,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Will, perhaps, in years to come,
آ آ آ آ Remember old Jack Palmer
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And the Old Rum Of Rum.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! the Old Keg of Rum! the Old Keg of Rum!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Remember old Jack Palmer
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  And the Old Keg of Rum.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton), titled "The Old Keg Of Rum", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
